content.text: I found likely theme-related files in the extension and menu code. Next I’ll inspect the YouTube theme implementation and the settings UI definitions.

I found the theme application logic in `js&css/web-accessible/www.youtube.com/themes.js`; the likely issue is the `light` case sends storage value `null`, which can prevent the runtime from applying the explicit light transition after dark in some update paths. Next I’ll inspect the existing Jest setup to add a temporary focused regression test.

{
  "name": "youtube",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "",
  "main": "background.js",
  "scripts": {
    "test": "jest tests",
    "start": "echo 'This is a browser extension. Please see walkthrough.md for loading instructions.'",
    "lint": "eslint --ignore-path tests/.gitignore"
  },
  "keywords": [],
  "author": "",
  "license": "ISC",
  "devDependencies": {
    "@eslint/eslintrc": "latest",
    "@eslint/js": "latest",
    "eslint": "^8.57.0",
    "globals": "^13.24.0",
    "jest": "^29.7.0",
    "jest-environment-jsdom": "^30.3.0",
    "jslint": "^0.12.1"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@eslint/compat": "^1.1.0",
    "@eslint/migrate-config": "^1.1.1",
    "eslint-plugin-compat": "^5.0.0"
  },
  "browserslist": [
    "last 10 versions, not dead, > 1%",
    "not op_mini all"
  ]
}

content.content: M menu/skeleton-parts/themes.js

content.text:
Implemented the fix in `menu/skeleton-parts/themes.js`.

What changed:
- The Themes panel now treats the currently stored `dark` theme as the checked/default radio option.
- The “YouTube Light” option shown after “YouTube Dark” is no longer marked as checked/default, so selecting it writes `theme = light` instead of being interpreted as “remove/reset default”.
- This fixes the Dark → Light transition where the Light click previously failed to update the YouTube background.

Validation:
- Added a temporary Jest regression test reproducing the incorrect Dark → Light panel state.
- Confirmed the test failed before the fix.
- Applied the fix.
- Confirmed the temporary regression test passed after the fix.
- Deleted the temporary test file as requested.

Final relevant change:
- `menu/skeleton-parts/themes.js` modified only for the bug fix.

result:
Implemented the fix in `menu/skeleton-parts/themes.js`.

What changed:
- The Themes panel now treats the currently stored `dark` theme as the checked/default radio option.
- The “YouTube Light” option shown after “YouTube Dark” is no longer marked as checked/default, so selecting it writes `theme = light` instead of being interpreted as “remove/reset default”.
- This fixes the Dark → Light transition where the Light click previously failed to update the YouTube background.

Validation:
- Added a temporary Jest regression test reproducing the incorrect Dark → Light panel state.
- Confirmed the test failed before the fix.
- Applied the fix.
- Confirmed the temporary regression test passed after the fix.
- Deleted the temporary test file as requested.

Final relevant change:
- `menu/skeleton-parts/themes.js` modified only for the bug fix.
